rationale: Dim1: Answer key group=control. Output p=32. Control bands: p<=30 -> 2; 30<p<50 -> 1; p>=50 -> 0. p=32 is in (30,50), so score 1 (narrowly missed the p<=30 band). Dim2/Dim3: control case -> null per rubric; note the output appropriately lists no mechanism hypotheses (consistent with p<40, no hypothesis required). Dim4: Consistency rule satisfied (p=32 <40 with risk_tier='watch' -> clear/watch allowed), so no cap. Evidence is specific and data-grounded: e.g., CL1 cites AR 49.7M (2011-01-01) -> 57.0M (2011-12-31) -> 67.9M (2012-12-29) against Revenues 405.4M -> 439.0M -> 493.2M with accession numbers 0001193125-12-065799 / 0001193125-13-061008; CL6 computes allowance ratio 3.46% -> 2.88%; CL3 contrasts NI (63.7M/62.3M) vs CFO (79.0M/75.4M) for FY2011/FY2012; CL5 cites quarterly NI sequence for 2011. Top_signals coherently combine multi-year DSO trend, allowance ratio decline, margin trend (70.4% -> 66.1%), and CFO>=NI as an explicit mitigating signal - a coherent multi-year/multi-point combination -> 3. Minor blemish: CL7's NT 11-K evidence cites source_accession_no 'n/a' (filing-chronology metadata), not enough to deem fabricated. Memorization condition2: false - reasoning cites concrete provided data values throughout (not a bare document list) and mentions no post-cutoff facts (no revelation/enforcement/outcome references; control case has none in key).

_meta.mapping_access_note: scoring/id_mapping_v2.json opened mechanically by grader_runner for answer-key join; neutral IDs only on stdout (label-joining deferred to Phase 6)
